introduction: This spec defines the workspace entity for the agent coordination system â€” a workspace maps to a git repository (and optional branch), is owned by a user, and optionally belongs to a team, providing the foundational context in which agent work is performed. Note: no Verified External API section is present in the PRD; requirements referencing Echo, Cobra, or SQLite APIs are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.--branch: CLI flag specifying the optional git branch or revision for the workspace being created.
glossary.--git-url: CLI flag specifying the git remote URL for the workspace being created.
glossary.--slug: CLI flag specifying the unique human-readable identifier for the workspace being created.
glossary.--team: CLI flag specifying the team slug for the workspace being created; resolved to a team UUID by the CLI before the API call.
glossary.API key: A per-user authentication credential passed in request headers to authenticate REST API calls.
glossary.GET /api/v1/teams: The REST endpoint used by the CLI to look up a team by slug for team UUID resolution.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces: The REST endpoint for creating a new workspace resource.
glossary.active: The default workspace status indicating the workspace is in normal use.
glossary.admin token: An API authentication token that operates with system-level privileges but is not associated with a real user account; cannot be used to create workspaces.
glossary.afc workspace create: The CLI subcommand for creating a new workspace, part of the `afc` Cobra CLI tool.
glossary.architecture/services-architecture.md: The canonical architecture document describing the operational store schema and service design for this system.
glossary.archived: A workspace status indicating the workspace is no longer active (reserved for future lifecycle operations).
glossary.branch: An optional git branch name or revision stored on a workspace record. NULL means the repository's default branch will be used.
glossary.created_at: The UTC timestamp automatically set when a workspace record is inserted.
glossary.git_url: The Git remote URL associated with a workspace. Accepts HTTPS (`https://...`) or SSH (`git@host:path`) format; not validated for reachability at creation time.
glossary.owner_id: Foreign key referencing `users(id)`; the user who created the workspace and is its owner.
glossary.personal workspace: A workspace with no team association (`team_id` IS NULL), owned solely by the creating user.
glossary.slug: A human-readable, URL-safe identifier: lowercase alphanumeric characters and hyphens, 3â€“64 characters, must start with a letter. Used to uniquely identify a workspace.
glossary.standard error envelope: The JSON error response shape used project-wide: `{"error": {"code": "<status>", "message": "<message>"}}`.
glossary.status: The lifecycle state of a workspace record. Valid values are `active` and `archived`; defaults to `active` on creation.
glossary.team membership: The association between a user and a team, granting the user a role (reader, editor, or admin) within that team.
glossary.team_id: Optional foreign key referencing `teams(id)`; associates a workspace with a team. NULL means a personal workspace.
glossary.team_rename spec: Spec 06 (`team_rename`) that renames the old `workspaces` table to `teams`, freeing the `workspaces` table name for this spec.
glossary.teams: The database table storing team (organizational group) records, renamed from 'workspaces' by the team_rename spec (06).
glossary.users: The database table storing user account records, referenced by `owner_id`.
glossary.workspaces: The database table storing workspace entity records, introduced by this spec.
glossary.id: UUID primary key field that uniquely identifies a workspace record in the `workspaces` table.
glossary.CreateWorkspace: Go store method that accepts workspace field values, generates a UUID, inserts a record into `workspaces`, and returns the populated workspace struct or an error.
glossary.os.Exit: Go standard library function (`os` package) that terminates the process immediately; store and handler code must never call it directly.
glossary.log.Fatal: Go standard library logging function that prints a message and then calls `os.Exit(1)`; store and handler code must never call it directly.
glossary.git@host:path: SSH-format git remote URL pattern (e.g. `git@github.com:org/repo.git`), one of the two accepted `git_url` formats.
glossary.https://: HTTPS URL scheme prefix; one of the two accepted `git_url` format prefixes for workspace creation.
glossary.afc workspace: CLI subcommand group under the `afc` Cobra CLI tool that groups workspace-related commands (e.g. `create`).
glossary.git@<host>:<path>: Parameterized SSH-format git URL pattern used in validation rules to match SSH remote URLs.
glossary.git://: Git protocol URL scheme; rejected by the `git_url` validator as an unsupported format.
glossary.http://: HTTP (non-TLS) URL scheme prefix; rejected by the `git_url` validator because only HTTPS is accepted.
glossary.ssh://: SSH URL scheme prefix; rejected by the `git_url` validator as an unsupported format (only `git@host:path` SSH syntax is accepted).
Shorthand notation for an HTTPS-format git remote URL, used in descriptions to represent any URL starting with `https://`.

user_story.goal: persist workspace records in a dedicated table with all required fields and constraints
user_story.benefit: workspace data is durably stored and queryable for downstream features

acceptance_criteria.action: defines a `workspaces` table with columns: `id` (TEXT PRIMARY KEY, UUID), `slug` (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL), `git_url` (TEXT NOT NULL), `branch` (TEXT nullable), `owner_id` (TEXT NOT NULL, foreign key referencing `users`(id)), `team_id` (TEXT nullable, foreign key referencing `teams`(id)), `status` (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'), `created_at` (DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
enforces the UNIQUE constraint on `slug` and NOT NULL constraints on `id`, `slug`, `git_url`, `owner_id`, `status`, and `created_at`
sets the default value of `status` to `active` and the default value of `created_at` to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP so application code need not supply these on insert

edge_cases.action: rejects the insert with a UNIQUE constraint violation error without modifying existing rows
edge_cases.return_contract: SQLite UNIQUE constraint failed error returned to the caller
edge_cases.error_condition: an INSERT is attempted with a `slug` that already exists in `workspaces`

edge_cases.action: DDL creation of `workspaces` fails with a foreign key reference error, blocking startup until the dependency is satisfied
edge_cases.return_contract: database error returned to application startup; process does not start
edge_cases.error_condition: the `team_rename spec` migration has not yet run and the `teams` table does not exist

requirements.title: Workspace store CRUD â€” create operation

user_story.goal: insert a new workspace record into the database via a store function
user_story.benefit: the REST handler and other callers have a single, tested insertion path

acceptance_criteria.action: exposes a `CreateWorkspace` function (or method) that accepts workspace field values, auto-generates a UUID for `id`, sets `status` to `active` and `created_at` to the current UTC time if not provided, inserts the record into `workspaces`, and returns the fully populated workspace struct
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns `(*Workspace, error)` â€” populated struct on success, nil and error on failure

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a sentinel duplicate-slug error (distinct from generic database errors) so the caller can map it to HTTP 409
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns nil and a typed duplicate-slug error
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a `CreateWorkspace` call is made with a `slug` that already exists in `workspaces`

acceptance_criteria.action: never calls `os.Exit` or `log.Fatal`; all errors are returned as Go error values to the caller

edge_cases.action: returns a wrapped database error immediately without retrying, leaving no partial state
edge_cases.return_contract: returns nil and a wrapped error describing the database failure
edge_cases.error_condition: the SQLite database is locked or unavailable when `CreateWorkspace` is called

edge_cases.action: returns a foreign key constraint error to the caller without inserting a workspace row
edge_cases.return_contract: returns nil and a foreign key violation error
edge_cases.error_condition: the provided `team_id` does not reference an existing row in `teams`

user_story.goal: create a new workspace by posting its details to the REST API
user_story.benefit: workspaces can be registered programmatically by tools and CI systems

acceptance_criteria.action: validates the request body, calls `CreateWorkspace` on the workspace store, and responds with the created workspace object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid authenticated request with `slug` and `git_url` is received

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request using the `standard error envelope`
rejects the request using the `standard error envelope`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `slug` field does not match the required format (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, 3â€“64 chars, starts with a letter)

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request using the `standard error envelope`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `git_url` field is not a valid HTTPS URL (starting with `https://`) or SSH URL (matching `git@host:path` pattern)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a conflict error using the `standard error envelope`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `slug` is valid but already exists in `workspaces`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a not-found error using the `standard error envelope`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `team_id` is provided but does not correspond to an existing team in `teams`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns a forbidden error using the `standard error envelope`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with `{"error": {"code": "403", "message": "not a member of this team"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `team_id` is provided and the team exists but the authenticated user is not a member of that team

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request using the `standard error envelope`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with `{"error": {"code": "403", "message": "workspace creation requires user authentication"}}`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the request is authenticated with an `admin token` (no real user context)

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `owner_id` to the authenticated user's ID derived from the `API key` credential; the request body never supplies `owner_id`
accepts `branch` and `team_id` as optional fields; if omitted or null, stores NULL in the `workspaces` table and reflects null in the response JSON
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON response fields `branch` and `team_id` are null when not provided

edge_cases.action: returns a bad request error using the `standard error envelope` without attempting database access
edge_cases.error_condition: the request body is malformed JSON or has an incorrect Content-Type

edge_cases.action: the Echo framework's request context cancels the operation; the handler returns a 500 error and logs the timeout without leaking goroutines
edge_cases.error_condition: the database call to `CreateWorkspace` hangs or takes longer than the configured request timeout

edge_cases.action: rejects the request at the authentication middleware layer before reaching the handler logic
edge_cases.error_condition: no `API key` header is present in the request

user_story.goal: create a workspace from the command line using human-readable flags including a team slug
user_story.benefit: workspaces can be registered without needing to know team UUIDs or craft raw HTTP requests

acceptance_criteria.action: registers as a Cobra subcommand under `afc workspace` with required flags `--git-url` and `--slug`, and optional flags `--branch` and `--team`
calls `GET /api/v1/teams` with a slug filter to resolve the team slug to a team UUID, then includes the resolved UUID as `team_id` in the `POST /api/v1/workspaces` request body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: `--team` flag is provided with a team slug value

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the created workspace object as pretty-printed or compact JSON to stdout and exits with code 0
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stdout contains the workspace JSON object; exit code 0

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a human-readable error message to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code without calling `POST /api/v1/workspaces`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stderr message identifying the unresolved team slug; exit code 1
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `--team` slug cannot be resolved (team not found in `GET /api/v1/teams` response)

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error message from the `standard error envelope` to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stderr contains the error message; exit code 1
acceptance_criteria.trigger: the `POST /api/v1/workspaces` call returns an error response

acceptance_criteria.action: Cobra prints usage help and an error message before any API calls are made
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: stderr contains usage text; exit code 1
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a required flag (`--git-url` or `--slug`) is missing

acceptance_criteria.action: never calls `os.Exit` directly in library or handler code; process termination is delegated to the Cobra command runner

edge_cases.action: returns an error from the HTTP client to the command handler, which prints a human-readable error to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code; no retry is attempted
edge_cases.return_contract: stderr describes the network failure; exit code 1
edge_cases.error_condition: the `GET /api/v1/teams` call for team slug resolution times out or the server is unreachable

edge_cases.action: returns the HTTP client error to the command handler, which prints a human-readable error to stderr and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: stderr describes the network failure; exit code 1
edge_cases.error_condition: the `POST /api/v1/workspaces` call times out or the server is unreachable

edge_cases.action: prints a descriptive error to stderr indicating an unexpected server response and exits with a non-zero exit code
edge_cases.return_contract: stderr describes the parse failure; exit code 1
edge_cases.error_condition: the API response body cannot be parsed as valid JSON (unexpected server response)

user_story.goal: find accurate descriptions of the team and workspace entities and their schemas
user_story.benefit: the canonical architecture documentation matches the implemented system, preventing confusion

acceptance_criteria.action: replaces all uses of 'workspace' that refer to the organizational/team concept with 'team', consistent with the `team_rename spec`

updates the workspace definition to describe a workspace as an entity that maps to a git repository (plus optional `branch`), is owned by a user (`owner_id`), optionally belongs to a team (`team_id`), and has a `status` of `active` or `archived`

updates the operational store schema section to include both the `teams` table schema (as renamed from the old workspaces table) and the new `workspaces` table schema with all columns defined in 07-REQ-1.1

user_story.goal: enforce a consistent slug format for workspaces at every entry point
user_story.benefit: slugs are URL-safe and human-readable, preventing downstream errors in routing and display

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts a `slug` only if it matches: starts with a letter [a-z], followed by lowercase alphanumeric characters or hyphens, total length 3â€“64 characters
rejects slugs that start with a digit or hyphen, contain uppercase letters, contain consecutive hyphens, end with a hyphen, or are shorter than 3 or longer than 64 characters
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns false for any invalid slug

edge_cases.error_condition: an empty string is passed as `slug`

user_story.goal: accept only well-formed git remote URLs at workspace creation time
user_story.benefit: prevents obviously invalid URLs from being stored while avoiding latency from reachability checks

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts URLs that start with `https://` (HTTPS format) or match the SSH pattern `git@<host>:<path>` (SSH format)
rejects local filesystem paths, `http://` (non-TLS), `git://`, `ssh://` scheme URLs, and any other format not matching HTTPS or SSH patterns
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns false for any non-matching URL

acceptance_criteria.action: does not perform any network call or DNS resolution â€” validation is purely syntactic

edge_cases.error_condition: an empty string is passed as `git_url`

correctness_properties.title: Slug uniqueness invariant
correctness_properties.invariant: no two records share the same `slug` value

correctness_properties.title: Owner always a real user
correctness_properties.invariant: `owner_id` references a valid row in `users` and was set from an authenticated non-admin user's ID

correctness_properties.title: Status default invariant
correctness_properties.invariant: `status` is `active` immediately after creation and is never null

correctness_properties.title: Team membership required for team association
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace record in `workspaces` with a non-null `team_id`
correctness_properties.invariant: the `owner_id` user was a member of the team identified by `team_id` at the time of workspace creation

correctness_properties.title: git_url format is always valid
correctness_properties.invariant: `git_url` matches the HTTPS (`https://...`) or SSH (`git@host:path`) pattern and is never null or empty

correctness_properties.title: slug format is always valid
correctness_properties.invariant: `slug` matches the pattern: starts with [a-z], contains only lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens, length 3â€“64

correctness_properties.title: Store never terminates process
correctness_properties.for_any: call to the workspace store from any caller
correctness_properties.invariant: the store returns errors as Go error values and never calls `os.Exit` or `log.Fatal`

execution_paths.title: Create workspace via REST API (happy path)

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/workspaces with a valid API key header and JSON body containing `slug`, `git_url`, and optionally `branch` and `team_id`
validates the API key, resolves the authenticated user, and verifies the caller is not an admin token
parses the request body, validates `slug` format via the slug validator, validates `git_url` format via the `git_url` validator
if `team_id` is provided, verifies the team exists in `teams` and that the authenticated user is a member of that team
calls `CreateWorkspace` with the validated fields, auto-generates a UUID for `id`, inserts a row into `workspaces` with `status` = `active` and `created_at` = now
responds with HTTP 201 and the fully populated workspace JSON object to the API client

execution_paths.title: Create workspace via CLI with team slug resolution

steps.action: runs `afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo.git --slug my-api --team my-team`
calls `GET /api/v1/teams` with a slug filter `my-team` to resolve the team slug to its UUID
returns the matching team record from `teams` including its UUID
constructs the create request body with `slug`, `git_url`, and the resolved `team_id` UUID, then calls `POST /api/v1/workspaces` with the user's `API key`
validates the API key and resolves the authenticated user
validates inputs, checks team membership, and calls `CreateWorkspace` on the workspace store
inserts the workspace row into `workspaces` and returns the populated workspace struct
prints the returned workspace JSON to stdout and exits with code 0
sends `POST /api/v1/workspaces` with a `slug` that already exists in `workspaces`
passes the validated request to `CreateWorkspace` on the workspace store
detects the UNIQUE constraint violation on `slug` and returns a typed duplicate-slug error
maps the duplicate-slug error to HTTP 409 and responds with the `standard error envelope` message `workspace slug already exists`
sends `POST /api/v1/workspaces` authenticated with an `admin token`
identifies the credential as an `admin token` (no real user context)
rejects the request and responds with HTTP 403 and `standard error envelope` message `workspace creation requires user authentication`

error_handling.condition: POST /api/v1/workspaces request body is missing `slug` or `git_url`
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 400 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "400", "message": "missing required fields"}}`

error_handling.condition: `slug` field does not match the required format
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 400 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "400", "message": "invalid slug format"}}`

error_handling.condition: `git_url` field is not a valid HTTPS or SSH git URL
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 400 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "400", "message": "invalid git_url format"}}`
return HTTP 409 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "409", "message": "workspace slug already exists"}}`

error_handling.condition: `team_id` is provided but does not match any row in `teams`
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 404 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "404", "message": "team not found"}}`

error_handling.condition: `team_id` is provided, team exists, but authenticated user is not a member of the team
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 403 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "403", "message": "not a member of this team"}}`

error_handling.condition: request is authenticated with an admin token (no real user context)
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 403 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "403", "message": "workspace creation requires user authentication"}}`

error_handling.condition: CLI --team flag slug cannot be resolved via GET /api/v1/teams
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints human-readable error to stderr identifying the unresolved team slug and exits with code 1 without calling POST /api/v1/workspaces

error_handling.condition: CLI POST /api/v1/workspaces returns an error response
error_handling.behavior: CLI prints the error message from the standard error envelope to stderr and exits with code 1

error_handling.condition: workspace store CreateWorkspace call encounters a database lock or unavailability
error_handling.behavior: returns a wrapped database error to the caller; no retry; no partial state left; handler maps to HTTP 500

error_handling.condition: request body is malformed JSON or has incorrect Content-Type
error_handling.behavior: return HTTP 400 with standard error envelope: `{"error": {"code": "400", "message": "missing required fields"}}` without attempting database access

error_handling.condition: no API key header is present in the POST /api/v1/workspaces request
error_handling.behavior: authentication middleware rejects the request with HTTP 401 and standard error envelope before reaching the handler
